message:
security(checkpoint,cli): fail closed on corrupt/symlinked checkpoints, reject ambiguous ID prefixes in history and summary

Hardening the checkpoint/storage subsystem and CLI lookup paths:

**Checkpoint selection: fail closed on corrupt entries (storage.go, capture.go):**
GetLatest and GetByIndex rewritten to use new getByRecentIndex that
walks entries sorted by modification time. If an invalid (corrupt)
checkpoint sits newer than the target, it blocks selection and returns
an error rather than silently returning an older valid one. GetLatest
now delegates to getByRecentIndex(1).

**Symlink rejection in incremental paths (incremental.go):**
incrementalDir on both IncrementalCreator and IncrementalResolver now
validates that neither the incremental/ base directory nor any individual
incremental subdirectory is a symlink. Signatures changed to return
(string, error).

**CLI history: ambiguous prefix detection (history.go):**
historyEntryByIDPrefix extracted and made to count all matches; ambiguous
prefixes return an error. Numeric-looking IDs that are out-of-range now
fall back to prefix matching. Session filter args normalized via
resolveHistorySessionFilter (project-scoped prefix expansion).

**CLI summary: project-scoped prefix normalization (summary.go):**
regenerateSummaryFromArchive resolves abbreviated project-scoped session
names before searching for archive files.

**Tests:** New cases cover corrupt-newer-checkpoint blocking, symlink
base/leaf dir rejection, ambiguous ID prefixes, and project prefix
normalization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
